pages.raw_text:
CONCONULLY, July 23.-The
history of the fight between Yaki-
ma City and North Yakima in the
sos promises to be repeated in
Oroville and New Oroville, and the
old town is fast being removed to
the new site. The Northern Pa
cific had hand in the Yakima
affair and men connected with the
Great Northern are said to be the
moving spirit in the Okanogan
county game.


Judge NJ. j. Gordon, C. A. Muir
ray and other Spokane people re-
Gently plated a new townsite a
half mile from Oroville. Old town
began proceedings for incorpora-
ton. Then the courts enjoined the
county commissioners from can
vassing the vote.


The next move was the Offering
to old town business people of ad-
vantageous situations in the new
town. The offers were accepted
and there has been an exodus to
the new town sufficient to depop-
ulate the old town. Those inter-
ested heavily in old town property
say that they will apply to the
courts for a revision of the pro-
ceedings.
